title: Run Hermes inventory
description: Discover Hermes paths, state DB shape, cron availability, Obsidian notes, and runtime tools.

title: Build dashboard shell
description: Create Python server, local JSON data files, and first clean dark dashboard UI.

title: Begin Phase 3 Hermes search and config viewer
description: Add read-only message-level search over Hermes state.db and a masked Hermes config/model viewer in Settings.

title: Improve Phase 3 session reader and observed-agent analytics
description: Highlight message search terms, jump to the matched message inside a focused conversation window, and add historical session analytics without claiming live agent tracking.

title: Add reproducible dependency manifest for Agent OS
description: Create a requirements.txt or pyproject.toml that captures Agent OS runtime dependencies, especially Google Calendar integration packages, so setup is reproducible and not tied to Brandon's current machine.

title: Upgrade dashboard health from soft heartbeat to real subsystem health
description: Make /api/health report meaningful status for state.db readability, config readability, calendar auth or fallback state, cron/jobs availability, and degraded conditions instead of always reporting healthy.

title: Add behavior-oriented regression tests for key dashboard flows
description: Supplement string-contract tests with higher-value endpoint, data-shape, and interaction-focused checks for calendar payloads, attention resolution, project hydration, and other critical read-only flows.

title: Add safer local server startup and shutdown handling
description: Add a safer local Agent OS server lifecycle workflow with preflight port checks, runtime state tracking, and stop/status helpers so stale listeners and restart conflicts are easier to resolve while keeping the dashboard local-only.

title: Tighten Hermes launcher and Python listener lifecycle coupling
description: Completed as far as project-owned scope can reasonably go: Mentat now has explicit launch/stop/status helpers, runtime state tracking, and launcher/listener diagnostics. Any remaining Windows mismatch between Hermes-managed background sessions and the real Python listener should now be treated as an upstream Hermes/process-manager issue rather than an active Mentat cleanup task.

title: Reduce monolith risk in server.py and app.js without changing scope
description: Completed the first scoped light modularization slice with commit 06036c0: runtime config parsing moved to runtime_config.py, shared frontend helpers moved to public/core.js, and fast user-facing Mentat rename landed while keeping repository/module/internal identifiers compatible for now.

title: Choose the next small Mentat modularization slice
description: Selected and completed the next small Mentat modularization slice: extracted /api/health subsystem diagnostics from server.py into health_checks.py while preserving endpoint contracts and avoiding new product scope.

title: Extract Mentat health checks into a dedicated module
description: Completed in commit 1dbe09b: extracted /api/health subsystem checks from server.py into health_checks.py with preserved contracts, focused regression tests (5/5), and full suite lock step (38/38).

title: Build runtime config foundation and audit hardcoded local assumptions
description: Add a layered Agent OS runtime config system (shared TOML defaults, local override TOML, env vars, and CLI flags), move key local path assumptions out of code, and document which Brandon-specific defaults remain intentional.

title: Build Agent Pulse 2.0 heartbeat registry
description: Completed: Agent Pulse now has a project-owned live heartbeat registry with GET /api/agents, POST /api/agents/heartbeat, data/agents.json, running/idle/blocked/done/failed state summaries, latest output/current task metadata, needs-user-input support, and a Today View panel that falls back to historical sessions when no live agents exist.

title: Wire live Agent Pulse heartbeat producers
description: Completed: Agent Pulse producer wiring is now rolled out through scripts/agent_heartbeat.py with one-shot beat, wrapped run, and examples output for ready-to-run producer commands. GET /api/agents now derives producer freshness, marks overdue active records stale instead of showing them live forever, and the Today View Agent Pulse panel surfaces producer guidance when the registry is empty.

title: Connect Google Calendar later
description: After the dashboard shell is stable, connect read-only Google Calendar integration via OAuth.

title: Finish Phase 2.5 UI/UX stabilization review
description: Review Today, Projects, and Tasks desktop/mobile UX after the command-center redesign; refine the connected header/task rail, project portfolio rail, dropdown behavior, screenshots, and any remaining redundancy before larger integrations.

title: Polish Google Calendar read-only integration
description: Turn the connected Google Calendar source into a useful Today/Calendar experience: today and 7-day agenda, fallback/stale states, event grouping, and read-only OAuth boundaries.

title: Build dashboard-native task creation and editing
description: Completed the first dashboard-native write-back slice: local-only validated task create/edit routes, Create Task from the queue header, Edit Task from the Selected Task inspector, delegated editor wiring, draft-state preservation, and project-owned data writes only. Dashboard-native project create/edit remains a separate later slice.

title: Evaluate Kanban or richer task board write-back
description: Completed evaluation for now: defer Kanban until the grouped queue + Selected Task inspector + native task editor + Agent Pulse registry have more real usage. If task volume or workflow friction appears, revisit a richer board as a project-owned API surface rather than Hermes core mutation.

title: Add read-only email pane after calendar stabilizes
description: Design a safe email panel for priority messages and needs-attention surfacing after calendar is stable; keep it read-only until Brandon explicitly approves send/delete actions.

title: Add Windows startup service docs and safe remote access option
description: Document running Agent OS on Windows login and evaluate Tailscale/VPN-only remote access; do not expose port 8888 publicly.

title: Reassess React migration only when interactions justify it
description: Keep the dashboard vanilla JS during UI stabilization; revisit React if direct editing, modals, routing, live agent roster, or websocket updates make the frontend too interaction-heavy.

title: Run Wilson Milsen review before the next major expansion
description: Before adding larger integrations, ask Wilson Milsen for a read-only YAGNI/code review of AGENTS.md, README.md, server.py, frontend files, data JSON, and the current diff; implement only high-impact safe suggestions.

title: Complete full Agent OS to Mentat rename boundary
description: Project/task data now use the Mentat project name. Keep the remaining full Agent OS to Mentat rename as a deliberate later milestone: repo path, helper script names, Python compatibility modules, broad docs/Obsidian note names, and compatibility aliases still need a planned migration instead of opportunistic cleanup.

title: Research next Mentat feature via Agent OS / agent dashboard landscape
description: Completed: created the Obsidian research note “Mentat - Agent OS Landscape Research - 2026-06-22” with YouTube/GitHub/product landscape findings and a recommendation to prioritize Agent Pulse, dashboard-native task intake, and structured run replay before email or a React migration.

title: Add dashboard-native project creation and editing
description: Later write-back slice: add local-only create/edit flows for projects backed by data/projects.json and the existing allowlisted dashboard API/data helpers. Keep task editing stable first and avoid broad architecture changes.

title: Build structured run replay / trace view for sessions
description: Completed first useful version: Agents / Sessions now has a Replay/Transcript tab toggle, read-only /api/hermes/sessions/<id>/replay parsing from Hermes state.db, medium-detail run summary, user intent, agent actions, error blockers, outcome, code/file summary, verification, inferred related tasks, and suggest-first/write-later copy. Agent-written summaries and task write-back remain later slices.

title: Making Test one
description: This is a test one!

title: Document website-to-agent messaging plan
description: Completed: created the website-to-agent messaging planning reference in Obsidian and repo docs, covering local queue/API, recipient model, Agent Pulse integration, safety boundaries, phased implementation, tests, and open decisions. Reference [[Mentat - Website-to-Agent Messaging Plan]] and docs/website-to-agent-messaging-plan.md before implementation.

title: Build website-to-agent messaging v1
description: Implement the first safe local-only website-to-agent messaging slice from [[Mentat - Website-to-Agent Messaging Plan]] / docs/website-to-agent-messaging-plan.md: project-owned message queue, POST/GET APIs, dashboard compose surface, Agent Pulse pending-message indication, and explicit safety/audit states. Do not allow arbitrary browser-triggered shell execution.

title: Add browser smoke tests before agent messaging
description: Add a lightweight headless browser smoke path before or alongside website-to-agent messaging. Cover Today render, nav, task create/edit control visibility, Agent Pulse empty/live states, task status filter, and the new agent message compose flow once implemented.

title: Organize compact board CSS by section
description: Cleanup after the approved compact dark redesign: keep the visual result unchanged while organizing public/styles.css into clear sections/tokens for shell, cards, Today, Projects/Tasks, Agents/Sessions, Calendar, forms, and responsive rules. Preserve the compact dark design contract.

title: Split app.js by view responsibility before heavier chat work
description: Reduce frontend hotspot risk before website-to-agent messaging by extracting or clearly isolating Today/tasks, Agents/Sessions, Calendar, Agent Pulse, Notes/Settings, and event wiring responsibilities. Preserve vanilla JS and existing DOM/API contracts.

title: Split server API domains before agent messaging grows backend scope
description: Plan and apply small backend extraction slices for project-owned API domains such as tasks, agents, Hermes sessions, calendar, and future agent_messages. Keep server.py routing simple, preserve endpoint contracts, and maintain Hermes-core read-only boundaries.

title: Run Wilson Milsen big-file review after compact redesign
description: Completed: Wilson-style read-only review of the current Mentat big-file hotspots was saved to docs/wilson-code-review-2026-06-25.md. Use it to prioritize cleanup before the website-to-agent messaging feature.

title: Fix Agent Pulse live visibility without manual heartbeat setup
description: Follow-up from Brandon noticing Agent Pulse appears not to work when no producer is emitting heartbeats. API/UI render correctly with a manual heartbeat, but the product needs clearer empty-state guidance and/or default Hermes producer wiring so active agents show up without manual commands. Keep writes project-owned and avoid Hermes core mutation unless a deliberate Hermes integration is approved.
